Summary: S1.1 API schema freeze and DTO package
Description: Define canonical OpenAPI v1 and shared DTO package for merged ai-enterprise-os + story-agent.
Acceptance Criteria: OpenAPI v1 committed; shared DTO package consumed by API and UI; branch policy contract documented

Summary: S1.2 Shared persistence and event stream
Description: Unify persistence model for stories, missions, decisions, conflicts, and observation events.
Acceptance Criteria: Supabase schema migrated; observation stream endpoint available; story lifecycle queryable end-to-end

Description: Implement prompt scopes (global/project/sprint/story/role) with version lineage and telemetry links.
Acceptance Criteria: Prompt render trace attached to mission runs; templates versioned; promote flow defined

Description: Translate crew personas into MCP role contracts with tool permissions and output schemas.
Acceptance Criteria: Role contract schema validated; unauthorized tool use blocked; role outputs schema-valid

Summary: S2.2 Conflict engine and admiral escalation
Description: Implement conflict state machine with arbitration and mandatory admiral escalation for unresolved conflicts.
Acceptance Criteria: Conflict transitions deterministic; escalation payload includes options/evidence; user decision unblocks execution

Description: Bridge mission outputs to branch/PR/revision lifecycle via deliver_mission_output and PR sync tools.
Acceptance Criteria: Single story can run from mission output to PR open; comment sync creates revision cycle

Summary: S3.1 Unified web + VS Code state sync
Description: Expose one API/event model to both web dashboard and VS Code extension with near-real-time state sync.
Acceptance Criteria: Story status change reflected in both clients within 2s; no duplicate business logic in clients

Summary: S3.2 Cost-aware model routing and policy gates
Description: Introduce Quark routing policy + Worf hard gates before PR-open and merge-ready states.
Acceptance Criteria: Model routing decisions logged with rationale; security gate failures block progression

Summary: S3.3 Pilot hardening and operational runbook
Description: Run pilot on 3 stories across 2 repos and capture cycle-time, quality, and escalation metrics.
Acceptance Criteria: 2+ stories merged through full loop; zero policy bypasses; runbook published
